title: The agreement between environmental advocates, the State Legislature- championed by Assemblymember Bauer-Kahan and Senator Glazer, the Governor and California Department of Parks a

raw_text:
```json
{
  "claims": [
    "Tesla Park is a 3,100-acre parcel of land located in eastern Alameda County that contains habitats for vulnerable species.",
    "Tesla Park includes sacred sites for Native people and historically significant mines.",
    "Tesla Park is at the intersection of biotic zones, resulting in rare and valuable diversity such as Blue Oak woodland and mountain savannah grassland.",
    "The Sierra Club California and its partners have opposed opening Tesla Park to off-highway vehicle recreational use.",
    "A court ruling determined that the environmental impact report for Tesla Park was inadequate, requiring State Parks to redo it.",
    "The agreement removes Tesla Park from the existing Carnegie State Vehicular Recreation Area and makes it an independent park.",
    "The agreement bans off-highway vehicles from ever accessing Tesla Park.",
    "The agreement aims to further the goals of the Governor’s October 2020 Executive Order to protect biodiversity and conserve 30 percent of California’s land and coastal waters by 2030.",
    "Funds will be transferred for the value of the land into the State Off-Highway Vehicle Fund for future development of an OHV recreation area elsewhere.",
    "The decision by the California Legislature and Administration to preserve Tesla Park represents a major victory for environmentalists across the state."
  ]
}
```
